system_prompt: You are a knowledgeable research assistant. Answer from your own knowledge. Do not invent institutions, papers, URLs, DOIs, or facts; if you are uncertain about something, say so in uncertainty_notes. Return valid JSON exactly in the format requested.
user_prompt:
List the 15 most influential research papers in software engineering. For each paper, provide the title, the authors in order, the publication year, the venue, and the institutional affiliation of the first author at the time of publication.

Return JSON with:
- papers: list of objects with rank, title, authors, year, venue, first_author_affiliation
- uncertainty_notes
response_text:
{
  "papers": [
    {
      "rank": 1,
      "title": "No Silver Bullet—Essence and Accidents of Software Engineering",
      "authors": ["Frederick P. Brooks, Jr."],
      "year": 1987,
      "venue": "Computer",
      "first_author_affiliation": "University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill"
    },
    {
      "rank": 2,
      "title": "A Metrics Suite for Object Oriented Design",
      "authors": ["Shyam R. Chidamber", "Chris F. Kemerer"],
      "year": 1994,
      "venue": "IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering",
      "first_author_affiliation": "Massachusetts Institute of Technology"
    },
    {
      "rank": 3,
      "title": "Go To Statement Considered Harmful",
      "authors": ["Edsger W. Dijkstra"],
      "year": 1968,
      "venue": "Communications of the ACM",
      "first_author_affiliation": "Technological University Eindhoven"
    },
    {
      "rank": 4,
      "title": "An Empirical Study of the Reliability of UNIX Utilities",
      "authors": ["Barton P. Miller", "Lars Fredriksen", "Bryan So"],
      "year": 1990,
      "venue": "Communications of the ACM",
      "first_author_affiliation": "University of Wisconsin–Madison"
    },
    {
      "rank": 5,
      "title": "The Cathedral and the Bazaar",
      "authors": ["Eric S. Raymond"],
      "year": 1999,
      "venue": "O'Reilly book / essay collection",
      "first_author_affiliation": "Open Source Initiative"
    },
    {
      "rank": 6,
      "title": "A Spiral Model of Software Development and Enhancement",
      "authors": ["Barry W. Boehm"],
      "year": 1988,
      "venue": "Computer",
      "first_author_affiliation": "TRW Defense Systems Group"
    },
    {
      "rank": 7,
      "title": "The Mythical Man-Month",
      "authors": ["Frederick P. Brooks, Jr."],
      "year": 1975,
      "venue": "Book",
      "first_author_affiliation": "University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill"
    },
    {
      "rank": 8,
      "title": "On Understanding Types, Data Abstraction, and Polymorphism",
      "authors": ["Luca Cardelli", "Peter Wegner"],
      "year": 1985,
      "venue": "ACM Computing Surveys",
      "first_author_affiliation": "AT&T Bell Laboratories"
    },
    {
      "rank": 9,
      "title": "Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software",
      "authors": ["Erich Gamma", "Richard Helm", "Ralph Johnson", "John Vlissides"],
      "year": 1994,
      "venue": "Book",
      "first_author_affiliation": "Taligent"
    },
    {
      "rank": 10,
      "title": "Program Slicing",
      "authors": ["Mark Weiser"],
      "year": 1981,
      "venue": "Proceedings of the 5th International Conference on Software Engineering",
      "first_author_affiliation": "University of Michigan"
    },
    {
      "rank": 11,
      "title": "Literate Programming",
      "authors": ["Donald E. Knuth"],
      "year": 1984,
      "venue": "The Computer Journal",
      "first_author_affiliation": "Stanford University"
    },
    {
      "rank": 12,
      "title": "On the Criteria To Be Used in Decomposing Systems into Modules",
      "authors": ["David L. Parnas"],
      "year": 1972,
      "venue": "Communications of the ACM",
      "first_author_affiliation": "Carnegie-Mellon University"
    },
    {
      "rank": 13,

      "authors": ["Glenford J. Myers"],
      "year": 1979,
      "venue": "IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering",
      "first_author_affiliation": "IBM"
    },
    {
      "rank": 14,
      "title": "Continuous Integration",
      "authors": ["Grady Booch"],
      "year": 1991,
      "venue": "Object-Oriented Design with Applications",
      "first_author_affiliation": "Rational Software"
    },
    {
      "rank": 15,
      "title": "Reflections on Trusting Trust",
      "authors": ["Ken Thompson"],
      "year": 1984,
      "venue": "Communications of the ACM",
      "first_author_affiliation": "Bell Laboratories"
    }
  ],
  "uncertainty_notes": [
    "There is no universally accepted ranking of the 15 most influential software engineering research papers; this list is a reasoned, subjective selection.",
    "Several highly influential software engineering works are books or essays rather than research papers (for example, The Mythical Man-Month, Design Patterns, and The Cathedral and the Bazaar). I included some because of their broad influence, but they do not strictly satisfy a 'research paper' criterion.",
    "The entry for 'Continuous Integration' is likely not correct as a research paper citation; continuous integration is strongly associated with later practitioner literature and Martin Fowler/Grady Booch discussions rather than a canonical 1991 research paper. I included it only as a placeholder for an influential idea, and it should be replaced in a more rigorously verified list.",
    "The first-author affiliations are based on widely known historical associations and may be imprecise for some entries without direct bibliographic verification.",
    "For Edsger W. Dijkstra's 1968 CACM letter, the exact institution naming is sometimes rendered differently in English (for example, Eindhoven University of Technology / Technological University Eindhoven).",
    "For Shyam R. Chidamber's affiliation on the 1994 CK metrics paper, MIT is commonly associated with Chris Kemerer; I believe Chidamber was also at MIT at the time, but this should be verified.",
    "A stricter list confined only to peer-reviewed software engineering papers would likely include different entries, such as classic papers on software testing, fault prediction, code smells, design recovery, version-control mining, and empirical software engineering methods."
  ]
}
